metrics.narrative: Jemena has articulated qualitative pain around manual procurement processes for P2P and S2C (source: Next Step 2026-06-19, quoting Rod Hughan). The buyer is 'keen on exploring P2P and S2C' and sees Zycus S2P suite as valuable (source: Eyhab Alkawasmi at Horizon AU 2026, per Next Step 2026-06-19). However, no baseline metrics have been quantified — no cycle time, error rate, headcount burden, or savings/ROI target captured. The business problem (manual, error-prone procurement) is confirmed; the financial impact is not. Quantifying the value case is the immediate next step to build a CFO-grade ROI argument and justify the $150k ACV investment against competing priorities.

Salesforce OpportunityContactRole — Courtney Joannidis Evaluator, Rod Hughan Decision Maker

champion.narrative: Champion strength is developing but unconfirmed. Eyhab Alkawasmi is the strongest advocate signal: he met the Zycus team at Horizon AU 2026 and proactively stated that Zycus S2P suite would be valuable to Jemena (source: Next Step 2026-06-19, via Adam Hasan). However, Eyhab's role, reporting line, and access to Rod Hughan (Decision Maker) or the Economic Buyer are unknown, and he was not present at the 2026-06-15 face-to-face. Courtney Joannidis (Group Manager Procurement Excellence and Delivery, Evaluator) is high-potential: she is operationally senior, engaged in the June meeting, and scoping integration and Merlin AI. But the relationship is early (connected only recently per Next Step), and there is no evidence yet of her coaching Zycus on internal politics, defending us against competitors (none named), or driving the agenda. Rod Hughan (Decision Maker) is accessible but his level of sponsorship (active push vs passive evaluation) is unclear. The next move is to deepen the Courtney relationship through a value-focused workshop and to map Eyhab's influence and access, converting one or both into an active, coaching champion who will advocate for Zycus in closed-door conversations.

competition.narrative: Competition is entirely unknown. No competitor is named in Salesforce Competitors__c, Next Step, Contact Roles, or the Avoma manifest (source: Salesforce ground truth, Competitors field unknown). It is unclear whether Jemena is running a sole-source evaluation (chose Zycus after Horizon advocacy and Rod's March outreach) or a multi-vendor RFP/shortlist where other P2P/S2C vendors (Coupa, SAP Ariba, Ivalua, GEP, Zip, Jaggaer) are being evaluated in parallel. The risk is high: if competitors are active and setting criteria, framing AI, or undercutting on price, Zycus is flying blind on positioning and may lose on factors we never see. The immediate move is to ask Rod Hughan and Courtney Joannidis directly in the next discovery call: 'Who else are you evaluating for P2P and S2C? What is driving you to look at alternatives now?' until we have a clear competitive landscape and can position Merlin AI and integration strength as differentiators.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is confirmed and owned. Rod Hughan (GM Procurement) stated that Jemena has manual processes for procurement and is keen on exploring P2P and S2C (source: Next Step 2026-06-19). Eyhab Alkawasmi (role unclear, met at Horizon AU 2026) reinforced that Zycus S2P suite would be valuable to Jemena and they are looking at S2C and P2P for their organization (source: Next Step 2026-06-19). The pain owner is Rod Hughan (Decision Maker, GM Procurement). The pain is operational: manual, likely error-prone and slow procurement workflows across source-to-contract and procure-to-pay. The buyer has self-identified the problem and the solution category (P2P/S2C automation plus Merlin AI). What is missing is the quantified impact (cost of manual process, error rate, cycle time) and the compelling event (what forces a decision by Feb 2027).

paper_process.narrative: Paper process is entirely undefined. No discussion of MSA, SOW, legal review, InfoSec questionnaire, procurement channel, or contracting mechanics has been logged. The 2026-06-15 meeting and Next Step focus on product scope and integration, not commercial or contracting. With a 2027-02-23 close date (237 days out), paper process clarity is not yet urgent, but as the deal moves to Shortlisted or Vendor Selected it will become critical. The move is to surface paper-process expectations during the next commercial conversation: does Jemena use a standard MSA template, what is InfoSec review lead time, is there a preferred procurement channel (direct vs reseller), and what is the signature authority threshold for a $150k ACV SaaS deal.

economic_buyer.narrative: Economic Buyer is unmapped. Rod Hughan holds the Decision Maker contact role and is GM Procurement, but a $150k ACV SaaS platform deal at a mid-sized infrastructure utility likely requires CFO or C-suite budget sign-off. Courtney Joannidis (Group Manager Procurement Excellence and Delivery, Evaluator role) and Alex Mackie (Manager Digital Procurement) are operational; neither controls commercial authority. No commercial or pricing conversation has been logged, and no executive sponsor (CFO, CIO, COO) is named in Contact Roles, Next Step, or the 2026-06-15 meeting. The immediate move is to ask Rod Hughan directly who approves the budget for a procurement transformation investment of this scale, then book a co-discovery call with that person to validate the business case and timeline.

decision_process.narrative: The decision process is emerging but not formalized. Rod Hughan (GM Procurement, Decision Maker) has been the relationship anchor since March 2026; Courtney Joannidis (Group Manager Procurement Excellence and Delivery, Evaluator) joined as the operational lead; Alex Mackie (Manager Digital Procurement) attended the 2026-06-15 meeting, signaling a multi-person evaluation team (source: Next Step 2026-06-19, Avoma manifest, Contact Roles). Eyhab Alkawasmi is described as an advocate post-Horizon AU 2026. The sequence so far: initial contact (Rod, March 2026) → Courtney connection → face-to-face scoping (June 2026) → Horizon event advocacy. What is missing: the formal approval chain (does Rod decide alone or escalate to CFO/C-suite?), whether this is sole-source or multi-vendor (no competitors named), timeline for demo/POC/evaluation phases, and who owns technical validation (IT/ERP team not yet surfaced). The next step is to co-author a mutual evaluation plan with Rod and Courtney, naming milestones, owners, and the final sign-off authority.

decision_criteria.narrative: Two explicit decision criteria have surfaced: integration with Jemena's current ERP and procurement systems, and Merlin AI capability (source: Next Step 2026-06-19, 'they want to integrate the software with their current setup and looking at Merlin AI as well'). The scope is P2P and S2C (iContract, iSupplier, eProcurement, Merlin per Products field). What remains undefined: the relative weight of AI vs core P2P/S2C functionality, whether this is a best-of-breed or single-suite evaluation, technical fit criteria (API standards, data governance, master data sync), compliance/InfoSec requirements, and commercial criteria (pricing model, term length, support SLA). The 2026-06-15 face-to-face was titled 'S2C & P2P' but no recording exists, so the depth of criteria discussion is unknown. The next move is a structured discovery session to map the full evaluation scorecard, confirm weighting, and understand how Merlin fits into their digital procurement roadmap.

Lock in the Merlin AI differentiation NOW while the buyer's interest is explicit and before competitors frame the AI narrative differently. Jemena stated they are 'looking at Merlin AI' (Next_Step__c), and Eyhab validated the S2P suite value at Horizon AU 2026. This is a rare early-stage window where AI is a stated need, not a late-stage upsell. Make Merlin the centerpiece of the discovery demo: show the negotiation agent on supplier price history, the contract intelligence auto-extraction, and the agentic procurement assistant that eliminates manual work (their core pain). If we let this become a generic P2P/S2C bake-off without the Merlin wedge, we lose differentiation and compete on price. Activation: Luke (owner) schedules a Merlin-led demo with Rod and Courtney within 14 days, and Product or a Merlin specialist co-presents.

Surface the competitive landscape and map the Economic Buyer in the same discovery conversation to control positioning before an RFP. We have zero competitive intel (no named rivals, unknown if sole-sourced or multi-vendor), and the EB (CFO or C-suite budget authority) is unmapped. Both gaps are high-risk in a $150k ACV enterprise deal: if competitors enter unseen, they may set evaluation criteria we don't know about; if the EB is invisible until commercials, we have no exec-level sponsorship to defend pricing or champion us in final approval. The move is surgical: in the next Rod + Courtney call, ask two direct questions: (1) 'Are you evaluating other vendors alongside us, and if so, who?' and (2) 'For a platform investment of this scale, who signs off commercially—does it need CFO or executive committee approval?' These answers unlock positioning and exec-threading strategy. Timing: next 7 days, before any formal RFP scoping.

Recruit Courtney Joannidis (Group Manager Procurement Excellence & Delivery, Evaluator role) as the operational champion by co-creating the quantified business case with her. She owns process improvement and delivery, and the pain (manual P2P/S2C) sits squarely in her remit. Right now we have qualitative pain but no quantified value (no baseline cycle time, FTE burden, maverick spend %, contract leakage, or ROI target). A champion is built by making them successful internally: help Courtney build the CFO-grade case (current-state cost + post-Zycus savings model + risk reduction + Merlin productivity multiplier) that she can take to Rod and the EB to justify the investment. This also deepens discovery, locks in criteria that favor Zycus, and gives Courtney ownership of the outcome. Activation: Luke works with Courtney to scope a joint ROI workshop within 21 days, using Zycus templates/benchmarks to quantify the manual-to-automated transformation.
